task:
ATOM 1 of ticket 0034 (priority creation must auto-register in order.json). Build the SINGLE shared registration owner — no wiring of call sites yet (later atoms do that).

FILE TO EDIT: packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts (the one owner of order.json + orphan logic).

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Extract the orphan-detection core so 'what is an orphan' has exactly one home. Today findOrphanedPriorities (line ~96) inlines: scan priorityMarkdownFiles, loadPriority for the id, skip section==='archive'|'backlog' || manifest.has(id) || allowlist.has(id). Factor that per-dir scan into one private helper (e.g. liveUnlistedPriorityIds(prioritiesDir): Promise<string[]>) and have findOrphanedPriorities call it so its behavior is byte-identical (its existing test in tests/priority-order.test.ts must stay green, unchanged).
2. Add and export an async function registerLivePriorities(prioritiesDir: string): Promise<string[]>. It must: read the current manifest (order.json) via the existing readManifest; compute the orphaned (live, top-level, loadable, non-archive/backlog, non-allowlisted, currently-unlisted) ids using the SAME shared helper from step 1; APPEND those ids to the existing manifest order (preserving existing order and any pre-existing ids, even ids whose files are now gone — do NOT drop them here; this function only ADDS missing live priorities, it is not a pruner); and write order.json atomically via the existing writeOrder/atomic-rename path. It must return the full updated order array. It must be idempotent (a second call with nothing new returns the same order and rewrites no new ids). When there are no orphans it still returns the current order (writing is a no-op-equivalent / harmless rewrite is fine, but prefer skipping the write when the order is unchanged).
   - IMPORTANT: do NOT reuse writePriorityOrder for the append, because writePriorityOrder filters requestedOrder through validIds and would silently drop ids whose files are missing. registerLivePriorities must preserve unknown/missing ids already in the manifest. Build the new order as [...existingManifest, ...newOrphansNotAlreadyListed] and pass it to the low-level writeOrder with a validIds set that includes BOTH the existing manifest ids AND the live ids (so nothing legitimately present is filtered out). Confirm by reading writeOrder that your validIds set prevents any drop of existing entries.

TESTS: add cases to packages/daemon/tests/priority-order.test.ts (do not weaken existing ones):
- registerLivePriorities appends an unlisted live priority id and leaves order.json with it present; findOrphanedPriorities then returns [] for that dir.
- idempotent: calling registerLivePriorities twice yields the same order and adds no duplicates.
- preserves existing order and does not drop a pre-existing manifest id whose file is absent.
- respects archive/backlog/allowlist (those are never auto-registered).

EXIT CRITERIA / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: the daemon package test suite is green (discover and run the daemon vitest suite, e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` or the repo's equivalent — report the exact command and its exit code/output), AND typecheck passes for the daemon package. The existing findOrphanedPriorities governance tests must stay green unchanged. Do NOT modify routes.ts, launcher.ts, or any Play this atom — wiring comes in later atoms. WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts and packages/daemon/tests/priority-order.test.ts only.

Report: the diff, the exact test command run, its real exit code, and the relevant test output lines proving the new tests pass.

ATOM 2 of ticket 0034. Wire the daemon createPriority route (Spine 1) to the registration owner built in atom 1, so the route cannot land a priority file without registering it in order.json — atomically in the same governance commit.

FILE: packages/daemon/src/routes.ts — function createPriority (around line 661). It currently: writes the .md, validates, renames into place, then `commitGovernance(ctx, ws.path, [relative(ws.path, target)], ...)`. It never touches order.json.

CHANGE:
1. Import registerLivePriorities from './priority-order.js' (it is already the import source for readPriorities/writePriorityOrder — add the new name to that existing import).
2. After the priority file is renamed into place and validated (i.e. after `await rename(tmp, target)` and the post-rename validateCreatedPriority), and BEFORE commitGovernance, call `await registerLivePriorities(prioritiesDir(ws.path))`. This writes the new id into order.json atomically via the owner.
3. Include order.json in the SAME commit so the file + registration land together: change the commitGovernance file list to `[relative(ws.path, target), relative(ws.path, priorityOrderFile(ws.path))]` (priorityOrderFile helper already exists at line ~84). commitGovernance/git add of order.json is correct because registerLivePriorities will always add the just-created id (it was absent from the manifest), so order.json genuinely changes.
4. Do NOT reimplement any orphan/append logic in routes.ts — call the owner only. Keep the existing tmpDir cleanup, 409 collision check, and error handling intact. Keep the success response shape.

TESTS (daemon suite — the create-priority route is exercised in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts; add there, do not weaken existing cases):
- Positive: POST create a priority through the route, then read cocoder/priorities/order.json (or via readPriorities) and assert the new id is present (registered, no manual step). Assert findOrphanedPriorities returns [] for that dir after creation.
- Negative/guard: assert the route cannot land a priority .md while leaving order.json unchanged — e.g. capture order.json before and after and assert it changed to include the new id (this is the test the ticket requires: an authoring path cannot land a priority file while leaving order.json unchanged).
- Confirm the committed file set / receipt for the create includes order.json (if the test asserts committedSha or committed paths, include order.json among them).

EXIT CRITERIA / MUST NOT BREAK: full daemon vitest suite green (run it; report exact command, real exit code, and the new test output lines) and `tsc --noEmit` for the daemon package exits 0. The existing priority-order governance test and all mutations tests stay green. Do NOT touch the authoring-Play path (launcher.ts/create-priority.md) — that is atom 3. WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/routes.ts and packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts only.

Report: the diff, exact test command, real exit code, and test output proving the new positive + negative tests pass.

ATOM 3 of ticket 0034. Wire the authoring-Play gate-commit spine (Spine 2) to the registration owner so a Play-authored priority is registered in order.json BY CONSTRUCTION (even when the headless Play LLM only writes the .md), and align the create-priority Play prose. Call the owner; do not reimplement orphan logic.

FILES:
1. packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts
   - HeadlessCommitOptions (interface ~line 1528): add an optional field `readonly beforeCommit?: () => Promise<void>`.
   - runHeadlessThenGateCommit (~1547): after the successful-turn path (i.e. after the `if (turn.exitCode !== 0)` early-return block, and BEFORE `const gate = await gateCommitRepair(...)` at ~1581), invoke the hook: if `opts.beforeCommit` is set, `await opts.beforeCommit()`. If it throws, do NOT commit — return a failure body in the same shape as the exitCode!=0 path (ok:false, committedPaths:[], commitSha:null, outOfLanePaths:[], the turnLogPath, a clear error string e.g. `Authoring Play registration step failed: <detail>`), append the audit and emit the event with status 'failed', exactly like the other failure return. Wrap just the beforeCommit call in try/catch for this.
   - requestAuthoringPlay (~1508, the call that builds the runHeadlessThenGateCommit options): pass `beforeCommit` ONLY for the priority-authoring plays (playId is one of 'create-priority','edit-priority','archive-priority' — NOT 'create-ticket'). The hook calls `await registerLivePriorities(join(workspace.path, 'cocoder', 'priorities'))`. Import registerLivePriorities from './priority-order.js' (oz-host.ts already imports from there; launcher needs its own import). registerLivePriorities is idempotent and only ADDS live unlisted priorities, so it is safe/no-op for edit-priority and archive-priority. order.json is inside the Play writeScope (cocoder/priorities/**), so gateCommitRepair (commitOnlyScope) will include it in the same commit automatically — confirm by reading the create-priority Play writeScope and gateCommitRepair behavior.

2. packages/personas/base/plays/create-priority.md — prose only. Currently step 6/closing says the dispatch harness commits the file and tells the author not to run git/commit. Update so it states that order.json registration is OWNED BY THE DISPATCH SPINE (the harness reconciles order.json automatically after the Play returns) — the Play author does NOT manually edit order.json and there is NO orphan/draft state. Keep it terse, keep one owner, do not duplicate the registration rule into the Play as a procedure the LLM must perform. Do not broaden writeScope. (This is base governance shipped to every workspace; this verified run is the sanctioned route per the ticket — run the Play tests below.)

TESTS — packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts (do not weaken existing cases):
- The existing create-priority dispatch tests (e.g. 'dispatches create-priority and commits the priority file through the repair spine' ~line 46) will now also commit cocoder/priorities/order.json — update their committed-paths assertions to include 'cocoder/priorities/order.json' (in the gate's reported order).
- Add an assertion (in an existing create-priority dispatch test or a new one) that after dispatch, cocoder/priorities/order.json exists and contains the new id (e.g. 'alpha'), and findOrphanedPriorities(<prioritiesDir>) returns [] — proving the spine registered it with NO manual Play step (the fake agent only writes the .md). Import findOrphanedPriorities from '../src/priority-order.js'.
- If any core Play-content test (e.g. packages/core/tests/priority-authoring-plays.test.ts) pins create-priority.md wording you changed, update it to match the new prose without weakening its intent; run it.

EXIT CRITERIA / MUST NOT BREAK: full daemon vitest suite green AND, if you touched core Play-content tests, the core suite for that file green. tsc --noEmit exits 0 for any package you edited (daemon; core if touched). Existing mutations/priority-order/authoring-play tests stay green. WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts, packages/personas/base/plays/create-priority.md, and (only if needed for the pinned-content test) packages/core/tests/priority-authoring-plays.test.ts.

Report: the diff, exact test command(s), real exit code(s), and the test output proving the order.json-registration assertion and updated committed-paths assertions pass.

ATOM 4 (final) of ticket 0034. Amend ADR-0038 to flip the recorded stance from DETECT to PREVENT, now that order.json registration is owned by the write spine (atoms 1-3 landed: registerLivePriorities in packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts is called by the daemon createPriority route and by the authoring-Play gate-commit spine, so no code path can create an orphan).

FILE: cocoder/decisions/0038-priority-visibility-invariant.md (governance prose; follow the existing ADR format/voice of its siblings in cocoder/decisions/).

REQUIRED CONTENT CHANGES:
1. Decision section: change the stance from 'detect-and-fail via findOrphanedPriorities + CI' to PREVENT-BY-CONSTRUCTION. State that the one write/commit spine that lands a cocoder/priorities/*.md also registers the id in order.json atomically in the same commit, via the single owner registerLivePriorities (packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts). Name the two real spines that call it: the daemon createPriority route, and the authoring-Play gate-commit (runHeadlessThenGateCommit beforeCommit hook for the priority-authoring Plays). So a priority cannot be created unregistered.
2. Keep findOrphanedPriorities + its daemon governance test as the explicit belt-and-suspenders net, and state that under normal operation it must now NEVER trip (it is a backstop, no longer the primary mechanism).
3. Reconcile with ADR-0010 and ADR-0025 explicitly: order.json remains an order-only overlay at RUNTIME serving (ADR-0010 — readPriorities still appends unlisted .md, existence still lives in the .md files), but REGISTRATION now happens at write time as part of ADR-0025's atomic validate->write->commit dispatch (order.json registration lands in the same governance commit, not a later manual step). Do not contradict ADR-0010's existence-ownership or ADR-0025's one-commit-spine.
4. Update the header metadata to record the amendment (e.g. extend the Status/Amends lines to note this run flips detect->prevent and that order.json registration is now owned by the write spine, dated 2026-06-23). Keep it terse and consistent with sibling ADRs. Prefer amending 0038 in place (the ticket allows amend OR superseding ADR; amend-in-place is the simpler, one-owner choice — do that unless you find a concrete reason a new ADR id is needed).
5. Consequences section: update bullets to reflect prevention (priority creation registers by construction; orphan guard is now a backstop that should never fire; runtime overlay unchanged).

MUST NOT BREAK: if any test pins ADR/decisions content (check packages/core/tests/drift-read-claims.test.ts and any decisions-referencing test), keep it green — run the affected suite and report the command + exit code. If no test pins this file, say so explicitly and run packages/core test (or the drift test) to confirm nothing regresses. Do NOT change product code in this atom (atoms 1-3 already did the behavior). WRITE SCOPE: cocoder/decisions/0038-priority-visibility-invariant.md only (also cocoder/decisions/README.md ONLY if it carries a one-line index row that must reflect the amended title/stance — otherwise leave it).

Report: the diff, whether any test pins the file, the exact test command run, and its real exit code/output.
